palette_hint: Warm off-white background (#FAF6F0), dark charcoal text (#2B2B2B), burnt sienna accent (#C0522B) for headings and section labels, with soft tan (#D9C4A8) for horizontal rule color.
type_style: Georgia serif for headings at font-weight 700 for a rustic warmth, system-ui sans-serif for body text at font-weight 400, moderate 1.6 line-height for easy reading.
description: Ember & Rye Kitchen is a single-recipe showcase site presenting a definitive slow-braised short rib with smoked paprika and rye whiskey glaze, laid out as a clean vertical card experience across five focused pages.
constraints: All page content is centered in a single column with max-width 640px and horizontal auto margins.
Page padding is 24px on the sides and 48px top and bottom on all pages.
Section headings use the burnt sienna accent color (#C0522B) at font-size 1.1rem uppercase letter-spacing 0.08em.
Thin horizontal rules (1px solid #D9C4A8) separate every major section with 32px vertical margin above and below.
Buttons and tag labels use a solid burnt sienna (#C0522B) background with white text, no border-radius, padding 10px 22px, font-size 0.95rem.

page_specs.featured-recipe: Displays the recipe title 'Slow-Braised Short Rib with Smoked Paprika & Rye Whiskey Glaze' in a large Georgia serif heading, followed by a two-line description of the dish, prep time (25 min), cook time (3 hrs 30 min), and serves (4 people) listed as simple labeled rows. A solid burnt sienna button labeled 'View Ingredients' links to the ingredients page.
page_specs.ingredients: Lists all ingredients in two labeled groups — 'For the Braise' (bone-in short ribs, beef stock, smoked paprika, garlic, shallots, tomato paste, fresh thyme) and 'For the Rye Glaze' (rye whiskey, brown sugar, whole-grain mustard, apple cider vinegar) — each item on its own line prefixed with a dash. A thin rule separates the groups, and a button at the bottom links to the method page.
page_specs.method: Presents the cooking steps as a numbered list with six clearly written steps covering searing the ribs, building the braise base, adding stock and aromatics, oven-braising at 325°F, reducing the rye glaze on the stovetop, and finishing the ribs with the glaze before serving. Each step is separated by a small vertical gap of 16px for readability.
page_specs.tips-and-swaps: Offers four concrete cook's notes under the heading 'Tips & Ingredient Swaps': substituting bourbon for rye whiskey, using a Dutch oven versus a slow cooker with adjusted timing, resting the ribs in braising liquid overnight for deeper flavor, and a gluten-free swap for the thickener. Each tip is introduced by a bold inline label in charcoal.
page_specs.about-the-cook: A short third-person bio of fictional recipe author Maren Solís — a former line cook from Albuquerque who now develops recipes focused on slow-cooked, smoke-forward comfort food. Includes her philosophy on braising, a note that this short rib recipe took eleven test batches to finalize, and a closing line inviting readers to write in with their own variations.
